methods.description: This method describes the process of measuring pollen using a volumetric method with a Hirst-type sampler and analyzing the data based on various variables such as pollen season duration, seasonal pollen integral, maximum daily concentration, and threshold levels for allergy symptoms.

steps.description: Pollen measurements were performed using the volumetric method, with a Hirst-type sampler operating in a continuous volumetric mode.
steps.searchable_summary: measure pollen using a Hirst-type sampler in continuous volumetric mode

steps.description: Counts were recorded over 7-day cycles and analyzed separately for each 24-hour period.
steps.searchable_summary: record and analyze pollen counts over 7-day cycles and 24-hour periods

steps.description: The analysis was based on the duration of the pollen season, determined by the 98% method, where the beginning and the end of the season fell on days with 1% and 99% of the annual total pollen catch, respectively.
steps.searchable_summary: determine pollen season duration using the 98% method

steps.description: Sum of the daily average pollen concentrations over the season expressed by the SPI (seasonal pollen integral).
Maximum daily pollen concentration in the season and peak date.
steps.searchable_summary: identify maximum daily pollen concentration and peak date

steps.description: The number of days with above-threshold pollen level, which was adopted according to the literature.
steps.searchable_summary: count days with above-threshold pollen levels
